response:
to emulate the delete confirmation page behavior before saving certain models in the admin, you can use a two-phase save approach. this involves first saving the parent model, and then checking if the changes were successful. if the save is successful, you can proceed to save the dependent models. if the save fails, you can rollback the changes and display a confirmation message. this approach ensures that the user is prompted for confirmation before any changes are saved.  

in django, you can implement this by overriding the save method of the parent model. within the save method, you can first attempt to save the parent model. if the save is successful, you can then save the dependent models. if the save of the parent model fails, you can rollback the changes and display a confirmation message. this approach allows you to control the flow of the save operation and provide the user with the necessary feedback.
